segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en:
Am I live yet? Yeah. Morning. 

We were a little worried today because we weren't sure, from the reservations, whether we could handle everybody. But it looks to me like there may be a couple of seats left up there. I think next year we’re going to have to find a different spot, because it looks to me like we’re up about 600 this year from last year. And to be on the safe side, we will seek out a larger spot. 

Certain implications to that, because some of the more experienced you know a few years ago we were holding this meeting at the Joslyn Museum, which is a temple of culture. [laughter] And we’ve now, of course, moved to an old vaudeville theatre. The only place in town that can hold us next year, I think, is the Ak-Sar-Ben Coliseum, where they have keno and racetracks. We are sliding down the cultural chain, just as Charlie predicted years ago. He saw this coming. [laughter]
segments.zh:
我在直播了嗎？嗯。早上好。 

今天我們有點擔心，因為從預訂的情況來看，我們不太確定能不能容納所有人。不過現在看起來上面可能還有幾個空位。我想明年我們可能得換個場地了，因為看起來今年的人數比去年多了大概600人。保險起見，我們會找一個更大的場地。 

一些联系是，更有经验的人應該記得幾年前我們是在喬斯林美術館舉辦這個會議的，那是文化的殿堂。[笑聲] 而現在，我們已經搬到了一個老式的雜耍劇院。至於明年，我想城裡唯一能容納我們的地方是阿克薩本體育館，那裡有基諾遊戲和賽馬場。我們一步步往文化的「下游」滑落，正像查理多年前預測的一樣。他早就看出來了。[笑聲]

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en:
I have some rather distressing news to report. There are always a few people that vote against everyone on the slate for directors. Maybe a dozen or so people do that, and then there are others that single-shot it. They pick out people to vote against. This will come as news to Charlie. I haven’t told him yet, but he is the only one among our candidates for directors that received no negative votes this year. [applauds] 

Hold, hold. No need to applaud. I tell you, when you lose out on the title of Miss Congeniality to Charlie, you know you are in trouble. [laughter]
segments.zh:
我有一些令人沮喪的消息要說。每年總會有幾個人，對董事候選名單上的每一位都投反對票。可能有十來個人會這麼做；另外一些人會「單點狙擊」，專門挑人來投反對票。這對查理來說可能是個新消息。我還沒告訴他，他是今年所有董事候選人當中，唯一沒有收到反對票的人。[掌聲]

等等、等等，不用鼓掌。我跟你說，當你在「最親切先生」這個頭銜上輸給查理時，你就知道自己麻煩大了。[笑聲]

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Now, I’d like to tell you a bit how we’ll run this. We’ll have the business meeting in a hurry, with the cooperation of all of you. We’ll introduce our managers who are here, and then we’ll have a Q&A period. We’ll run that until 12 o’clock, at which point we’ll break. Then, at 12:15, if the hardcore want to stick around, we will have another hour or so, until about 1:15, of questions. You’re free to leave, of course, any time. And I’ve pointed out in the past that it’s much better form if you leave when Charlie is talking rather than when I’m talking, but the…feel free anytime. If you’re panicked and you’re worried about being conspicuous by leaving, you will be able to leave at noon. We will have buses out front that will take you to the hotels or the airport…or to any place in town in which we have a commercial interest. [laughter] We encourage you staying around on that basis.
segments.zh: 現在我想簡單介紹我們會如何進行。在大家的配合下，我們會很快完成正式的會議。我們會介紹在場的經理人，接著進入問答環節。一直進行到中午12點，我們休息。在12點15分，如果有‘硬茬’繼續，我們有額外一個多小時的問答，到1點15分左右。當然，你們隨時可以離開。我過去提過，最好是在查理而不是我講話的時候離開，不過……隨時都可以。如果你緊張或擔心中途離場太顯眼，你可以在中午離開。我們在外面有巴士，可以送你們去飯店或機場……或者是城裡任何我們有商業興趣的地方。[笑聲] 基於這個原因，我們鼓勵大家留下來。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en:
Let’s get the business of the meeting out of the way, and we get onto more interesting things. I will first introduce the Berkshire Hathaway directors that are probably directors…that are present, in addition to myself. First of all, there’s Charlie, who is the vice chairman of Berkshire. And if the rest of you will stand…we have Susan T. Buffett, Howard Buffett, Malcolm Chase III, and Walter Scott Jr. And that’s it. [applauds] 

Also with us today are partners in the firm of Deloitte & Touche, our auditors Mr. Ron Burgess and Mr. Craig Christensen. They are available to respond to appropriate questions you might have concerning their firm’s audit of the accounts of Berkshire. Mr. Forrest Carter, secretary of Berkshire, will make a written record of the proceedings. Mr. Robert Fitzsimmons has been appointed inspector of election at this meeting. He will certify the count of votes cast in the election for directors. The named proxy holders for this meeting are Walter Scott Jr. and Mark D. Hamburg. Proxy cards have been returned through last Friday, representing 1,035,680 Berkshire shares to be voted by the proxy holders, as indicated on the cards. That number of shares represents a quorum, and we will therefore directly proceed with the meeting. We will conduct the business meeting and then adjourn the formal meeting. After that, we will entertain questions that you might have. In the minutes of the last meeting of shareholders, I recognise Mr. Walter Scott Jr., who will place a motion before the meeting.
segments.zh:
讓我們先把會議的事項處理完，然後再進入更有意思的部分。我將介紹在場除了我本人之外的伯克希爾・哈薩維董事。首先是查理，他是伯克希爾的副董事長。其他各位若方便的話請起立……蘇珊・T・巴菲特、霍華德・巴菲特、馬爾科姆・切斯三世，以及沃爾特・史考特二世。[掌聲]

今天和我們在一起的，還有德勤會計師事務所的合夥人：我們的審計師羅恩・伯吉斯先生和克雷格・克里斯滕森先生。他們可以回應其對伯克希爾帳目審計工作相關的問題。伯克希爾的秘書福雷斯特・卡特先生將對進程進行書面記錄。羅伯特・菲茨西蒙斯先生已被任命為本次會議的選舉監票人，他將負責認證董事選舉的投票結果。指定的委託投票代理人為沃爾特・史考特二世與馬克・D・漢堡先生。到上週五，已收到的委託投票卡共代表1,035,680股伯克希爾股份，依照卡片上的指示。該股份數量已構成法定出席人數，我們將正式開始會議。我們先進行會議的正式議程，之後宣告正式會議結束，回答各位的問題。在上一次股東大會的會議記錄中，我提名沃爾特・史考特二世先生向本次會議提出動議。

segments.speaker: Walter Scott Jr.
segments.en: I move that the reading of the minutes of the last meeting the shareholders be dispensed with.
segments.zh: 我動議免予宣讀上一次股東大會的會議紀錄。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Do I hear a second? The motion has been moved and seconded. Are there any comments or questions? Hearing none, we will vote on the motion by voice vote. [laughter] All those in favour, say “aye”. [Aye] Opposed? Motion’s carried and it’s a vote. Does the secretary have reported the number of Berkshire shares outstanding, entitled to vote, represented at the meeting?
segments.zh: 有人附議嗎？本動議已經提出並獲得附議。有沒有任何意見或問題？既然沒有，我們將以口頭表決的方式對此動議進行表決。[笑聲] 贊成的請說同意。[同意] 有無反對？動議通過，並據此表決通過。請問秘書是否已報告，在本次會議中出席並有表決權的伯克希爾流通在外股份數量？

segments.speaker: Forrest Carter
segments.en: Yes, I do. As indicated in the proxy statement that accompanied the notice of this meeting, that was sent by first-class mail to all shareholders of record on March 8, 1994, being the record date for this meeting, there were 1,177,750 shares of Berkshire common stock outstanding, with each share entitled to one vote on motions considered at the meeting. Of that number, 1,035,680 shares are represented at this meeting by proxies returned through last Friday.
segments.zh: 是的，我有。如隨本次會議通知一同寄發的委託書說明中所指出的，該報告已於1994年3月8日（本次會議的股權登記日）以第一類郵件寄送給所有在冊股東。當時，伯克希爾普通股流通在外共有1,177,750股，每一股在本次會議審議的動議中均有一票表決權。1,035,680股通過截至上週五回收的委託投票，於本次會議中獲得代表。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Thank you. We’ll proceed to elect directors. If a shareholder is present who wishes to withdraw a proxy previously sent in and vote in person, he or she may do so. Also, if any shareholder is present, has not turned in a proxy, and desires a ballot in order to vote in person, you may do so. If you wish to do this, please identify yourself to meeting officials in the aisles, who will furnish a ballot to you. With those persons desiring ballots please identify themselves so that we may distribute them. Just raise your hand. Okay. I now recognise Mr. Walter Scott Jr. to place a motion before the meeting with respect to the election of directors.
segments.zh: 謝謝。我們將進行董事選舉。若有在場股東希望撤回先前提交的委託書、改為親自投票，您可以這樣做。另外，若有在場股東未提交委託書，並希望取得選票以便親自投票，您也可以這樣做。若您希望這樣做，請向走道上的工作人員表明身分，他們會提供選票給您。需要選票的人士，請表明身分以便我們分發選票。舉手即可。好的。現在我請小沃爾特・斯科特先生就董事選舉事項提出動議。

segments.speaker: Walter Scott Jr.
segments.en: I move that Warren Buffett, Susan Buffett, Howard Buffett, Malcolm Chase, Charlie Munger, and Walter Scott be elected as directors.
segments.zh: 我提議選舉沃倫・巴菲特、蘇珊・巴菲特、霍華德・巴菲特、馬爾科姆・蔡斯、查理・芒格，以及沃爾特・斯科特擔任董事。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Is there a second? It has been moved and seconded that Warren Buffett, Susan T. Buffett, Howard J. Buffett, Malcolm G. Chase III, Charlie T. Munger, and Walter Scott Jr. be elected as directors. Are there any other nominations? Is there any discussion? The motion and nominations will now be acted upon. If there are any shareholders voting in person, they should now mark their ballots and allow the ballots to be delivered to the inspector of elections. Seeing none. The proxy holders please all submit to the inspector of elections the ballots voting the proxies in accordance with the instructions they have received. Mr. Fitzsimmons, when you’re ready, you may give your report.
segments.zh: 有人附議嗎？該動議已提出並獲附議：選舉沃倫・巴菲特、蘇珊・T・巴菲特、霍華德・J・巴菲特、馬爾科姆・G・蔡斯三世、查理・T・芒格，以及小沃爾特・斯科特擔任董事。還有其他提名嗎？是否有任何討論？現在對該動議及提名進行表決。若有任何股東親自投票，請現在在選票上作出標記，並將選票交給選舉監票人。看來沒有。請委託書持有人根據其收到的指示，將代表委託書投票的選票提交給選舉監票人。菲茨西蒙斯先生，準備好了的話，您可以作出報告。

segments.speaker: Fitzsimmons
segments.en: My report is ready. The ballots of the proxy holders received through last Friday cast not less than 1,035,407 votes for each nominee. That number far exceeds a majority of the number of all shares outstanding, and a more precise count cannot change the results of the election. However, the certification required by Delaware law regarding the precise count of the votes, including the votes cast in person at this meeting, will be given to the secretary to be placed with the minutes of this meeting.
segments.zh: 我的報告準備好了。截至上週五收到的委託投票，每位候選人皆獲得不少於 1,035,407 票，遠遠超過所有流通股的過半數。進行更精確的計票也無法改變選舉結果。然而，依據德拉瓦州法律所要求，關於精確票數的正式認證（包括本次會議中親自投下的票數）將提交給公司秘書，並列入會議紀錄。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Thank you, Mr. Fitzsimmons. Warren Buffett, Susan T. Buffett, Howard J. Buffett, Malcolm G. Chase III, Charlie T. Munger, and Walter Scott Jr. have been elected as directors. [applauds] Does anyone have any further business to come before this meeting before we adjourn? If not, I recognise Mr. Walter Scott Jr. to place a motion before the meeting.
segments.zh: 謝謝您，菲茨西蒙斯先生。沃倫・巴菲特、蘇珊・T・巴菲特、霍華德・J・巴菲特、馬爾科姆・G・蔡斯三世、查理・T・芒格，以及小沃爾特・斯科特已當選為董事。［掌聲］在我們休會前，是否還有其他事項需要提交處理？若沒有，請小沃爾特・斯科特先生向會議提出一項動議。

segments.speaker: Walter Scott Jr.
segments.en: I move the meeting be adjourned.
segments.zh: 我提議本次會議休會。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Second? The motion to adjourn has been made and seconded. We will vote by voice. Is there any discussion? If not, all in favour say “aye.” [aye] Opposed, say “no.” The meeting is adjourned.
segments.zh: 有人附議嗎？休會動議已提出並獲附議。我們將以口頭表決方式進行投票。是否有任何討論？若沒有，贊成者請說「同意」。［同意聲］反對者請說「反對」。本次會議到此休會。［掌聲］

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: It’s democracy in Middle America. [laughter] Now, I’d like to introduce some of the people who make this place to you. If you would hold your applause until the end, because there are quite a number of our managers here. I’m not sure which ones for sure are here. Some of them may be out attending the store. But first of all, from the Nebraska Furniture Mart: Louie, Ron, and Irv Blumkin. I’m not sure who’s here, but would you stand, please? Any of the Blumkins that are present? Okay, looks like Irv's there. I can’t quite see. Yeah. From Borsheims, Susan Jacques is here. Susan? There she is. Susan had a record day yesterday. She just— [applauds] Susan became CEO just a few months ago, and she’s turning in records already. Keep it up. [laughter]
segments.zh: 這就是美國中部的民主。［笑聲］現在，我想向大家介紹一些成就這個地方的人。請大家把掌聲留到最後，因為今天有相當多的經理人在場。我不確定哪些人一定在場，有些人可能正在店裡忙。首先，來自內布拉斯加家具商場的路易、羅恩和艾爾夫・布魯姆金。我不確定誰在這裡，但請你們站起來好嗎？有任何布魯姆金家族成員在場嗎？好的，看起來艾爾夫在那邊，我看不太清楚。來自 Borsheims 珠寶店的蘇珊・賈克斯也在這裡。蘇珊？她在那裡。蘇珊昨天創下了單日紀錄。［掌聲］蘇珊幾個月前才成為 CEO，現在已經開始創紀錄了。繼續保持。［笑聲］

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: And from Central States Indemnity, we have the Kizers. I’m not sure which ones are here, but there’s Bill Sr., Bill Jr., John, and Dick Kizer. I think I can see John. Don Wurster from National Indemnity. Rod Eldred from the Homestate Companies. Brad Kinstler from Cypress, our workers’ comp company. Ajit Jain, the big-ticket writer in the East. And Mike Goldberg, who runs our real estate finance group and generally oversees the insurance group. Mike? Gary Heldman from Fechheimer. Chuck Huggins from See’s Candies. Stan Lipsey from The Buffalo News. Chuck’s been with us, incidentally, twenty-odd years. Stan’s been working with me for well over twenty-five years.
segments.zh: 來自中部州保險公司的凱澤家族成員也來了。我不確定具體是哪幾位，但肯定有老比爾和小比爾、約翰和迪克。我能看到約翰。來自國家保險公司的唐・伍斯特。來自家園保險公司的羅德・艾爾德雷德。來自賽普拉斯公司（我們的工傷賠償公司）的布拉德・金斯特勒。東部地區負責大額保險業務的阿吉特・賈恩。以及負責我們房地產金融並統籌保險業務的邁克・戈德堡。邁克？來自費希海默公司的加里・赫爾德曼。來自希氏糖果公司的查克・哈金斯。來自《布法羅新聞報》的史丹・利普西。順便一提，查克在我們公司工作了二十多年。史丹和我共事也超過二十五年了。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Frank Rooney and Jim Issler from H.H. Brown. Dave Hillstrom from Precision Steel. Ralph Schey from Scott Fetzer. Peter Lunder, who was with our newest acquisition, Dexter Shoe, and Harold Alfond, his partner, couldn’t be with us because his wife is ill. And finally, the manager who’s been with Charlie and me the longest, Harry Bottle from K&W. Harry?
segments.zh: 來自H.H.布朗鞋業公司的法蘭克・魯尼和吉姆・伊斯勒。來自精密鋼鐵公司的戴夫・希爾斯特羅姆。來自斯科特・費策爾公司的拉爾夫・謝伊。還有彼得・倫德，他來自我們最新收購的德克斯特鞋业公司。他的合夥人哈羅德・阿爾方德因為太太生病而無法出席。最後，還有與查理和我共事最久的經理人，來自 K&W 的哈利・博特。哈利？

segments.speaker: Charlie Munger
segments.en: There he is.
segments.zh: 他在那裡。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: There’s Harry, yeah. Harry saved our bacon back in 1962 or so, when in some mad moment I went into the windmill business, and Harry got me out of it. [laughter] That’s our group of managers, and I’d appreciate it if you’d give them a hand. [applauds]
segments.zh: 哈利在那裡。大概在 1962 年，哈利曾救了我們一命。當時我一時瘋狂跑去做風車生意，結果是哈利把我從裡面救了出來。［笑聲］以上就是我們的經理人團隊，如果各位願意給他們一些掌聲，我會非常感激。［掌聲］

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: I have one piece of good news about next year for you, in addition to moving to larger quarters. They are going to add non-stop air service from New York, Washington, and Los Angeles here in the next few months — Midwest Express. I hope they do very well with it, and I hope that makes it easier for you to get into town.
segments.zh: 除了明年將搬到更大的場地之外，我還有一個好消息。在接下來幾個月裡，中西部快運航空公司將新增從紐約、華盛頓和洛杉磯直飛這裡的航班。我希望他們的營運非常成功，也希望這能讓各位更方便來到小鎮上。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Now, for the next two hours and fifteen minutes or so, we’ll have a session where we will take questions. We have seven zones — three on the main floor. We’ll start over there with Zone One and work across the main floor. If you raise your hand, the person handling the microphone will pass it to you. We’ll try not to repeat on any individual in any one zone until everyone in that zone has had a chance to ask one question. So after you’ve been on once, let other people get a shot. When we move up to the loges, we have one person there. And in the case we have three in the balcony, which is essentially full now. Up there, we would appreciate it if you would leave your seat and go to the person with the microphone. It’ll be easier in both the loges and the balcony to handle it that way. If you go a little ahead of time, then if there’s a line of two or three, you can line up for questions in both the loge and the balcony.
segments.zh: 接下來大約兩小時十五分鐘，我們將回答問題。我們有七個區域，主會場有三個區。我們會從那邊的第一區開始，然後依序橫向進行。若您舉手，工作人員會把麥克風遞給您。在該區每個人都有機會提問一次之前，我們會盡量避免在同一區域內重複到同一個人。所以當您已經問過一次後，請讓其他人有機會發問。包廂區有一位工作人員。陽台區幾乎坐滿了，那裡會有三位工作人員。在那些區域，我們希望您能離開座位，走到拿著麥克風的人那裡。這樣包廂區和陽台區都會比較容易進行。如果您能稍微提前過去，若已有兩三人在排隊，您能在包廂區與陽台區排隊提問。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Whatever you’d care to ask — if you want an optimistic answer, you’ll of course direct your question to Charlie. If you’d like a little more realism, you’ll come to me. [laughter] Let’s start over in Zone One. Sometimes we can’t see too well from up here; in fact, I can’t even see the monitor right now. Do we have one over there? And if you’ll identify yourself by name and hometown, we’d appreciate it.
segments.zh: 無論想問什麼，如果您想要一個樂觀的答案，當然要問查理；如果你想要稍微現實一點的答案，那就來問我。［笑聲］我們從第一區開始。有時候我們從台上看不太清楚；事實上，我現在連屏幕都看不到。那邊有人嗎？如果提問時能先說明您的姓名和家鄉，我們會很感謝。

segments.speaker: Michael Mullen
segments.en: My name is Michael Mullen from Omaha. Would you comment on the use of derivatives? I noticed Dell Computer stock was off two and a half points Friday, with the losses of derivatives.
segments.zh: 我叫麥可‧穆倫，來自奧馬哈。您能否談談衍生金融工具？我注意到因為衍生品交易的虧損，戴爾電腦的股價週五下跌了2.5%。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en:
The question is about derivatives. We have in this room the author of the best thing you can read on that. There was an article in Fortune about a month ago by Carol Loomis on derivatives — by far the best article that’s been written. We also have some people in the room that do business in derivatives from Salomon. It’s a very broad subject. As we said last year, I think someone asked what might be the big financial story of the ’90s, and we said we obviously don’t know, but if we had to pick a topic, it could well be derivatives.

Because they lend themselves to the use of unusual amounts of leverage. They’re sometimes not completely understood by the people involved. Anytime you combine ignorance and borrowed money, you can get some pretty interesting consequences. [laughter]

Particularly when the numbers get big — and you’ve seen that recently with the Procter & Gamble announcement. Now, I don’t know the details of P&G’s derivatives, but I understand at least that what started out as interest-rate swaps ended up with P&G writing puts on large quantities of U.S., and I think one other country’s, bonds. Anytime you go from selling swaps to writing puts on bonds, you’ve made a big jump. [laughter] 

The ability to borrow enormous amounts of money, combined with the chance to get either very rich or very poor very quickly, has historically been a recipe for trouble at some point. Derivatives are not going to go away. They serve useful purposes and all that, but I’m just saying that they have that potential, and we’ve seen a little bit of that already. I can’t think of anything that we’ve done that would approach...would you think of anything we do that approaches derivatives surely? directly?
segments.zh:
這個問題是關於金融衍生品。關於這個，這個房間裡有一位寫過最佳文章的作者。大約一個月前，《財富》上刊登了卡羅爾·盧米斯寫的一篇關於衍生品的文章。毫無疑問，那是目前寫得最好的一篇。現場也有一些來自索羅門公司、從事衍生品業務的人。這是一個非常廣泛的主題。就像我們去年提到的，有人問：「90 年代最宏大的金融故事會是什麼？」我們說，我們當然不知道，但如果一定要選，那很可能是金融衍生品。

因為它們有著異常多的槓桿操作。有時候，連參與其中的人都未必完全理解它們。任何時候當你把「無知」和「借來的錢」結合在一起，你會得到相當有趣的後果。［笑聲］

尤其當金額非常巨大時。最近寶潔公司的公告就是個例子。我並不清楚 P&G 衍生品交易的細節，但據我所知，他們原本只是做利率互换（swap），最後卻演變成對大量美國以及另一個國家的債券賣出期權（puts）。任何時候當你從賣出掉期到賣出債券期權，那都是一個很大的跳躍。［笑聲］

能夠借到巨額資金，再加上可能在極短時間內暴富或变得一贫如洗，從歷史上看，那遲早都會帶來麻煩。衍生品不會消失。它們有其用途等等，但我只是想說，它們具有潛在風險，而我們已經看到一些例子了。我想不到我們做過任何使用衍生品......你能想到我們有什麼業務是直接使用衍生品的嗎？

segments.speaker: Charlie Munger
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 沒有。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: I may have to cut him off if he talks too long. [laughter] Is there anything you would like to add to your already extensive remarks?
segments.zh: 如果他講話太久我可能得打斷他。[笑聲] 在你足夠篇幅的評論外，你還想説點什麼嗎？

segments.speaker: Charlie Munger
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 不。

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Ok. In that case we'll go to zone two.
segments.zh: 好。既然如此，我們從區二繼續。

segments.speaker: Hugh Stevenson
segments.en: My name is Hugh Stevenson. I'm a shareholder from Atlanta. My question involves the company's investment in the stock of Cap Cities. It's been my understanding in the past that was regarded as one of the four "permanent holdings" of the company. I was a little bit confused by the disposition of one million shares. Could you clarify that? Was my previous understanding incorrect, or has there been some change or is there a third possibility?
segments.zh: 我的名字是休斯 · 斯提夫森。我是來自亞特蘭大市的一名股東。我的問題有關於公司對大都會的投資。我過去認為它是我們四個“永久倉位”之一。我對關於其一百萬股的減持感到疑惑。您能解釋一下嗎？是我之前的理解錯誤，或是情況發生了變化，或是有第三種可能？

segments.speaker: Warren Buffett
segments.en: Well, we have classified the Washington Post company, Cap Cities, Geico, and Coke in the category of permanent holdings. But in the case of three of those four. The Washington Post company, I don't know, maybe seven or eight years ago. Geico some years back.
